title: Generate, Validate, and Execute Easytrieve Programs

steps.title: Easytrieve Program Structure
hints: An Easytrieve Plus program has three main sections in order: FILE (input/output file declarations), W (working storage), and P (procedure logic). Each section begins with its keyword on a line by itself.

Lines starting with * or /* are comments. Easytrieve is not column-sensitive — statements can begin anywhere after column 1, but column 1 is reserved for statement labels and the continuation character.

steps.title: File and Field Definitions
hints: The FILE statement names the file and links it to its JCL DD name: FILE EMPLOYEE EMPLOYEE-DD. Immediately after, list each field with: name  start-position  length  type  [decimals]
Field types: A = alphanumeric, N = numeric (display), P = packed decimal. A salary field with 2 decimal places is defined as: EMP-SALARY  44  7  N  2

steps.title: Working Storage
hints: The W section declares your program's working variables. Each entry looks like: fieldname  type  length  [decimals]  — for example, TOTAL-SAL  N  11  2 defines an 11-digit numeric field with 2 decimal places.
Initialize a field at declaration time with the VALUE clause: COUNTER  N  6  VALUE 0. Without VALUE, numeric fields start at zero and alphanumeric fields start as spaces.

steps.title: Conditional Processing
hints: Easytrieve conditionals follow the pattern: IF condition / statements / ELSE / statements / END-IF. The condition uses EQ, NE, GT, LT, GE, LE for comparisons, and AND/OR to combine them.
To test whether a field contains a valid number, use: IF fieldname NUMERIC. To test a specific value: IF EMP-STATUS EQ 'A'. Literals are enclosed in single quotes.

steps.title: Generating Reports
hints: Define a report with: REPORT reportname  PRINTER. Then specify TITLE 'Report Title', HEADING 'col heading', and LINE field1  field2  field3 to define the output columns. Call PRINT reportname in the P section to write a line.

HEADING and LINE statements belong inside the REPORT block. The TITLE appears at the top of each page. Each LINE statement defines one output row layout — list the fields in the order you want them printed left to right.

steps.title: Arithmetic and Accumulators
hints: Use COMPUTE to perform arithmetic: COMPUTE TOTAL-SAL = TOTAL-SAL + EMP-SALARY. Operators are + (add), - (subtract), * (multiply), / (divide). Always define the result field in the W section first.

Easytrieve also provides built-in summary functions on REPORT fields: SUM fieldname accumulates a total automatically; COUNT counts records. These are specified on the LINE or FOOTING statement inside a REPORT block.

steps.title: Data Validation
hints: A validation program reads each record, applies checks, and writes errors to an error report. Use IF NOT fieldname NUMERIC to catch non-numeric data in a numeric field, and IF fieldname EQ SPACES to find missing required fields.
Keep an error counter in W: ERROR-COUNT  N  6  VALUE 0. Increment it with COMPUTE ERROR-COUNT = ERROR-COUNT + 1. Write a summary at end-of-file using the EOF label: LABEL EOF / PRINT SUMMARY-REPORT.

steps.title: JCL to Execute Easytrieve
hints: The Easytrieve Plus load module is EZTPA00. Execute it with: //STEP1 EXEC PGM=EZTPA00. Your Easytrieve source goes in the SYSIN DD statement (either inline with DD * or as a catalogued dataset).
Every DD name referenced in your FILE statements must have a matching DD card in the JCL. SYSPRINT DD SYSOUT=* captures the report output. EZTVARY DD is optional but holds Easytrieve system messages.
